steps.title: Set up the inequality for the width constraint
steps.content: If n parking spaces fit perpendicular to a 135-foot curb, each space has width $\frac{135}{n}$ feet. Since standard widths are between 7.5 and 9.0 feet, we write: $$7.5 \le \frac{135}{n} \le 9.0$$

steps.title: Solve the compound inequality for n
steps.content:
Left inequality: From $7.5 \le \frac{135}{n}$, multiply by n (positive) to get $7.5n \le 135$, then divide by 7.5 to get $n \le 18$.
Right inequality: From $\frac{135}{n} \le 9$, multiply by n to get $135 \le 9n$, then divide by 9 to get $15 \le n$.
Combining both results: $$15 \le n \le 18$$ That matches choice D — D is correct.
